picks.reason: Kelly’s xFIP is 4.17 but recent decline (L14 xFIP 5.94) vs. Phillips’ 3.88 xFIP; MIA holds a slight edge. MIA bullpen is fatigued (all three high-leverage arms used in last two days), which shrinks the gap under the 4‑pt threshold. No bet.

Both starters are below average (Matthews L14 xFIP 5.03, Montero 4.98). MIN’s bullpen (4.87 ERA) is shaky while DET’s unit is also below par. Estimated runs cluster around the posted total, and the ML market price (DET -123) closely mirrors a coin‑flip game after adjustments. No edge.

Initial team-quality models favored STL, but the massive bullpen disparity (NYM 2.95 vs. STL 3.83) swings the run projection back to NYM (≈4.0–3.7). Dobbins’ small sample adds uncertainty. STL +122 does not clear the 4‑pt gate once bullpen advantage is factored.

Both teams are close in true talent, and neither starter provides a decisive advantage (Rocker xFIP 4.36, Wacha 4.46). The total of 10.0 looks high, but insufficient data (wind unavailable, unclear bullpen situation due to KC’s reported 22.25 ERA) prevents a confident total play.

Coors Field run factor of 130 drives an expected total near 12.5 after adjusting for both starters’ xFIP (Cabrera 3.71 -> 4.82, Feltner 4.44 -> 5.77) and poor bullpens (both ERA >5.5 inflated). The -108 line implies 51.9% while the projected mean gives >61% likelihood. Two of five factors (park, bullpen) point firmly Over; no counteracting wind or elite starter presence.
picks.looked_up: verified park factor and wind (9.4 mph NE not strong enough to suppress scoring)

picks.reason: LAD is elite (pyth .728) and -161 is close to fair value despite PIT’s gaudy wRC+ 158 vs LHP. Wrobleski’s recent xFIP (2.33) offsets the platoon edge. Market price leaves no gap >4 pts on either side.

Bryan Woo’s xFIP (3.43) significantly out‑classes Bradish (3.68), and SEA’s offense (wRC+ 118 vs RHP) has a clear edge on BAL’s pitching. BAL’s bullpen (6.06 ERA) is a liability, while SEA’s fatigued arm Ferrer only costs ~0.5 runs. Run projection: SEA 4.47, BAL ~3.77, giving SEA a 58.5% win probability against an implied 51.7% at -107.
